topic: Why anxiety spikes then fades in sobriety

cover.headline: Why anxiety spikes then fades in [sobriety]
cover.subtitle: The science of how your brain recalibrates after quitting alcohol

slides.title: Why anxiety surges first (0-72 hours)
items: Alcohol artificially boosts GABA, your brain's calm signal
Your brain compensates by overproducing glutamate (excitement)
When alcohol stops, glutamate overwhelms the system, causing racing thoughts

slides.title: How anxiety fades by week 2
items: GABA receptors slowly upregulate, restoring natural calm
Glutamate levels normalize, reducing mental noise
Your HPA axis recalibrates, lowering cortisol production

end.saveLine: Save this if you're white-knuckling through sober [anxiety]
end.shareLine: Share with someone who drinks to quiet their [mind]
end.closer: The calm you're chasing is already inside you
